prompt: The code compiles with an unchecked cast warning and fails at runtime. What is the bug?
choices: ArrayList cannot hold both String and int values
Assigning a raw type to a parameterized type bypasses generics; strings.get(1) retrieves an Integer and the implicit cast to String fails
raw.add(42) should use Integer.valueOf(42) to store integers
List<String> requires all elements to be converted to String at insertion time
explanation: Using raw types (List without type parameter) bypasses compile-time generics checks. The raw list holds both String and Integer. Assigning to List<String> is allowed with a warning (heap pollution). strings.get(1) inserts an implicit cast to String, which fails for the Integer value 42 at runtime. Always use parameterized types.
